title: Starting a redis:6.0.1 container no longer works with podman 2.0.0-dev
body:
/kind bug

A redis 6.0.1 container no longer works with podman 2.0.0-dev. See the test below:

A test with Podman 1.9.3:

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.9.3
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

Start the redis 6.0.1 container:

```
$ podman run -d --rm --name redis-testing -p 6379:6379 redis:6.0.1
d625ee58cb991f5058dbd6028f0024eab9f12874fac5a8c8d3d0f9b546858c81
```

Test connecting to redis on localhost on port 6379:

```
$ redis-cli ping
PONG
```

Remove the `redis-testing` container:

```
$ podman stop redis-testing
d625ee58cb991f5058dbd6028f0024eab9f12874fac5a8c8d3d0f9b546858c81
```

Now upgrade to podman 2.0.0. 

```
$ podman version
Version:      2.0.0-dev
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.3
Git Commit:   2c532a92cd9993d93f010c736dd61102d269eece
Built:        Wed Jun 10 19:00:00 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Start the redis 6.0.1 container:

```
$ podman run --rm --name redis-testing -p 6379:6379 redis:6.0.1
41926f287f0ffe8a9476ea0ce9412596ad0456c681391d010157d9dfce903032
```

It will barf out a bunch of chown errors and the container refuses to start. See the [podman2rediserrors.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/4767943/podman2rediserrors.txt) file for the full error output.

```
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.note.Linux': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.strtab': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/__mcount_loc': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.exit.text': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.orc_unwind_ip': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.gnu.linkonce.this_module': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.symtab': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.rodata': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.init.text': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.note.gnu.build-id': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.text': Read-only file system
chown: changing ownership of './sys/module/nft_fib_inet/sections/.rodata.str1.1': Read-only file system
```

The redis 6.0.1 container has not started and you cannot connect to it:

```
$ redis-cli ping
Could not connect to Redis at 127.0.0.1:6379: Connection refused
```

comments.body:
It's in the entrypoint of the container.

```
# allow the container to be started with `--user`
if [ "$1" = 'redis-server' -a "$(id -u)" = '0' ]; then
	find . \! -user redis -exec chown redis '{}' +
	exec gosu redis "$0" "$@"
fi
```

Which is fatal because it's used with set -e.

Ideally, this find would also include the -xdev option so it didn't traverse /sys and /proc.  AFAIC that's a bug with the container image.

Proof:
Put this in fixed.sh
```
#!/bin/sh
set -e

# first arg is `-f` or `--some-option`
# or first arg is `something.conf`
if [ "${1#-}" != "$1" ] || [ "${1%.conf}" != "$1" ]; then
	set -- redis-server "$@"
fi

# allow the container to be started with `--user`
if [ "$1" = 'redis-server' -a "$(id -u)" = '0' ]; then
	find . -xdev \! -user redis -exec chown redis '{}' + || true
	exec gosu redis "$0" "$@"
fi

exec "$@"
```
chmod a+x fixed.sh
`podman run -d --rm --name redis-testing -v $PWD/fixed.sh:/usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh  -p 6379:6379 redis:6.0.1`
The || true could probably be removed with further tweaks, i.e. ignore the /sys folder, and maybe use -type f to exclude the broken links.
The real problem is podman is ignoring the WorkingDir of /data provided in the image.  It's starting in /, which is causing the problem.

Dockerfile:
```
FROM alpine

WORKDIR /tmp
```

And then
`buildah bud -t testimage .`

And
`podman run --rm -it testimage sh`
/ #

fail.
Vs:

`podman run --rm -it -w /tmp testimage sh`
/tmp #
